stories.headline: India secures massive $30B data center investment boosting global AI and cloud infrastructure

perspectives.label: Conservative
perspectives.body: India’s digital infrastructure journey is gathering remarkable momentum. AirTrunk has announced plans to invest around Rs. 3 lakh crore ($30 billion) in India, and develop 5 GW of data centre capacity.

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Japan is converting used cooking oil into sustainable aviation fuel to power greener flights. Cuba battles blackouts, shortages and a worsening economic crisis. India's economy grew 7.7%, but inflation and oil prices remain major concerns

stories.headline: Ukraine strikes Russian oil refinery 2000km deep in Tyumen

perspectives.label: Independent
perspectives.body: Incredible night for Ukraine, taking out Russian oil infrastructure and military targets from St Petersburg to the Urals. 2000km from Ukraine, in Tyumen, the Antipinsky Oil Refinery, capacity 9 million tons/year burns after early morning strikes.

stories.headline: AI buildout pressuring markets but strengthens Bitcoin case
stories.body: The AI buildout is absorbing capital at historic scale, creating temporary pressure across global markets. That does not weaken Bitcoin. It strengthens the case for scarce, liquid, digital capital. Bitcoin remains the premier asset for the long term. $BTC

stories.headline: Bitcoin long-term chart shows broken uptrend and potential head-and-shoulders top
stories.body: Look at this long-term Bitcoin chart. The uptrend off the Dec. 2022 low is broken. There's a huge head-and-shoulders top forming. The most likely resolution is a retest of the longer-term uptrend off the Dec. 2018 low. If it holds, that puts a bottom between $25,000 and $27,000.

stories.headline: Bitcoin integrates with global economy, currencies, credit, securities, and institutions.
stories.body: Bitcoin Capitalist: Bitcoin reaches its full potential by integrating with the global economy: currencies, credit, securities, companies, banks, institutions, governments, families, and individuals. Bitcoin is an open monetary network for everyone.

stories.headline: Border czar Tom Homan visits ICE facility for lunch
stories.body: "I was told the food was bad, so I sat in the cafeteria right along with the detainees." Border czar Tom Homan says he made a surprise lunch visit to an ICE detention facility after hearing complaints about the food. He says he had spaghetti and meat sauce, green beans, rolls and butter, and a fruit drink, but skipped the cake for dessert. "I couldn't finish it all," he said during an interview with @LaraLeaTrump.

stories.headline: Trump says team will explore US stakes in AI companies
stories.body: Donald Trump told reporters that his team might buy US stakes in artificial-intelligence companies and said he would host a meeting with AI executives as soon as next week

stories.headline: Pentagon raises counterintelligence threat level for Israel over eavesdropping concerns.
stories.body: The Pentagon has raised its counterintelligence threat level for Israel amid concerns about eavesdropping on U.S.-Iran talks. https://www.nytimes.com/2026/06/06/us/politics/pentagon-sees-growing-espionage-threat-from-israel.html?smtyp=cur&smid=tw-nytimes

stories.headline: All day Astronomy reports historic breakthrough in pancreatic cancer drug trial
stories.body:
🚨: Doctors erupt into a standing ovation over this historic breakthrough.

An experimental oral drug called daraxonrasib has delivered historic, 'grand slam' clinical trial results for advanced pancreatic cancer, slashing the risk of death by 60% compared to standard chemotherapy.

stories.headline: Yorba Linda California Temple milestone for local Latter-day Saints
stories.body:
Yorba Linda California Temple marks a significant milestone for local Latter-day Saints
After an effort to save a piece of Church land, members of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in southern California now rejoice at the Yorba Linda California Temple in their midst.

stories.headline: Thomas Massie comments on conspiracy theorists being right about NDAA
stories.body:
Thomas Massie says the conspiracy theorists were right again about Section 224 of the NDAA, which he says will merge the United States military with the IDF.
Massie says the provision is treasonous and warns that it will force the United States to compromise its own security for Benjamin Netanyahu's demands.
"The difference between a conspiracy theory in Washington has been reduced from six months to six days."

stories.headline: Runbeck whistleblower confirms illegal ballots inserted in 2022 Arizona election
stories.body:
Here it is:

RUNBECK WHISTLEBLOWER:

CONFIRMS BEYOND THE SHADOW OF A DOUBT,

That Runbeck printed approximately 300,000 ballots and illegally inserted them into the tally—not coming from drop boxes, not coming from polling locations, but instead being brought in by employees—for the Arizona 2022 General Election, effectively overthrowing the state government of Arizona.

📝Katie Hobbs and her co-conspirator, Adrian Fontes, rigged three elections in a row.
